seoTitle: Mount Eba vs Mount George — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Eba and Mount George. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Eba vs Mount George: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Eba and Mount George.
heading: Mount Eba vs Mount George — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Eba, South Australia and Mount George, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Eba has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Mount George (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Eba or Mount George safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Eba has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Mount George scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Eba scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Mount George scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Eba or Mount George?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Eba has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.32% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
